key_insight: ReverseMath turns existing math problems inside out by inverting the answer, creating new verifiable problems at scale and exposing memorization-like behavior in LLMs.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical way to generate labeled math-training data automatically while also providing a stronger evaluation probe for robust reasoning systems.
method: Mask a numerical value in an existing problem.
Use the original answer as a known condition.
Rewrite the problem so the masked value becomes the new answer.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports that paired original/reversed problems cause substantial behavioral shifts, with models sometimes failing on reversed versions and even outputting the original answer.
watch_item: Quality depends on whether the rewritten inverse problem remains mathematically valid and unambiguous, so inversion rules need careful checking.
paper_title: ReverseMath: Answer Inversion for Scalable and Verifiable Mathematical Problem Generation
